real_insight: Take any contract you own and walk it down nine reality-tests (uncleavable / incombustible / unwettable / undryable / eternal / all-pervading / stable / immovable / primordial). For each property, name the test that would falsify it. The properties for which you cannot name a falsifier are the ones your contract does not actually have.
objection: I went looking for the 'every API gets nine virtues' wisdom-theater move and the verse refuses it. The falsifiability field explicitly disqualifies the read where the nine properties are marketing labels: 'A contract is not eternal because it carries a version number; it is eternal if it actually survives the next migration.' The implication is operational and falsifier-driven: 'For each property, name the test that would falsify it.' The fintech API-governance scenario is concrete (32 contracts, named failures, named owners). The counter-example carves out deliberately substrate-coupled contracts (the materialised view honest about its Postgres dependence) so the nine-property checklist is not used as a universal reject-everything bludgeon. PASS.
